title: Directing the Architect of the Capitol to install at a permanent location on the western front of the United States Capitol an honorific plaque listing the names of all of the officers of the United States Capitol Police, the Metropolitan Police Department of the District of Columbia, and other Federal, State, and local law enforcement agencies and protective entities who responded to the violence that occurred at the United States Capitol on January 6, 2021.
plain_summary: This bill requires the Architect of the Capitol to put up a special plaque on the west side of the Capitol building. The plaque will honor all the police officers and law enforcement people who helped during the violent events at the Capitol on January 6, 2021. It will have all their names listed on it so people can remember what they did. The plaque must be installed within thirty days and will stay there permanently.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.

sections.title: Section 1 Installation of Plaque Honoring Law Enforcement Officers Who Responded to Violence at the Capitol on January 6, 2021
sections.text: Within thirty days after Congress passes this resolution, the Architect of the Capitol (the person who manages Capitol buildings and grounds) must put up a permanent honor plaque on the western front of the United States Capitol building. The plaque must follow the rules in section two hundred fourteen of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act of twenty twenty two. The plaque will list the names of all police officers who responded to the violence at the United States Capitol on January sixth, twenty twenty one. This includes officers from the United States Capitol Police, the Metropolitan Police Department of the District of Columbia, and all other federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies and protective groups who came to help that day.
